metadata.procedure_phase: Stage 4 OPERATIONALLY CLOSED at commit ecc8e7c (Stage 4 closure record + Phase C remediation verified + 1h45m soak CLEAN). EA-8 has retired correctly. Phase C remediation devplatform commits: `be22a15` (wake_launcher path resolution fix) + `751c3df` (handoff-prep pause). Stage 5 (Cleanup per 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml — MEDIUM risk, single_session) NOT YET SPAWNED. Guide-#9's first substantive task is the Variant B comprehension gate; second task is to consider a Stage 5.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint (per Guide-#7/#8 procedural-inheritance methodology); third task is to draft the Stage 5 EA-9 initialization pointer per the established pattern, AFTER LA pre-resolves 3 open scope decisions (see d4 below).
metadata.branch_at_handoff_blarai: chore/platform-extraction @ ecc8e7c (Stage 4 closure record commit; STATUS.md alone +121/-8; commit aggregate +409/-8 across 3 files: STATUS.md +121/-8, PHASE_B_FINDINGS_PLUS_PHASE_C_OPTIONS.xml +185/-0, PHASE_C_CLOSURE_SUMMARY.xml +103/-0; Stage 4 Closure Footnotes 8 items; Anomalies A15-A19 subsections; Phase C addendum to Stage 4 COMPLETE marker). 1 commit ahead of Stage 4 close marker 5965f6b. The handoff XML commit (8468f8f) advances HEAD; this corrections commit advances HEAD again. Guide-#9 should expect HEAD = handoff-corrections-commit at session start.
metadata.branch_at_handoff_devplatform: main @ 751c3df (handoff-prep pause; cumulative state since Stage 4 cutover at df3d940 via 48df457 + b9b213d + be22a15 + 751c3df). 5 commits since Stage 3 close (95884a0).
metadata.status_md_last_commit: ecc8e7c (Stage 4 close STATUS append: +121/-8 STATUS.md changes specifically; commit aggregate was +409/-8 across STATUS.md + 2 new XMLs at temp_for_responses/; Stage 4 Closure Footnotes section added with 8 items; A15-A19 anomalies added; Stage Ledger Stage 4 row PENDING → COMPLETE; Current State table updated for Stage 4 close + Stage 5 transition; Phase C addendum appended to existing Stage 4 COMPLETE marker; 2 Execution Log entries added for Stage 4 multi-phase + Stage 4 close commits)
metadata.fleet_pause_state: PAUSED at `devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` — fleet_paused=true, fleet_paused_reason='guide-8 to guide-9 handoff drafting', last_updated_by='guide_8_handoff', last_updated_utc='2026-04-29T15:24:23Z'. Pause committed at devplatform `751c3df`. NOTE: `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` is a DEAD ARTIFACT post-Phase-C4 (paused with stale BUG 5 reason from emergency re-pause earlier in Phase C; NOT read by wake_launcher anymore — wake_launcher line 809 fixed to read devplatform state.json via $PSScriptRoot derivation). Auto-resolved at Stage 5.1 deletion per Ticket KK.
metadata.worktree_state_blarai: Working tree NOT clean: (a) `M tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` — BlarAI dead-artifact runtime data (paused state from BUG 5 emergency re-pause; auto-resolved at Stage 5.1 directory deletion per Ticket KK; do NOT delete or commit pre-Stage-5.1). (b) `?? .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` — wake_launcher mutex lock file written every firing; should be added to .gitignore at Stage 5.7 per dispatch (Ticket bound under Stage 5.7 inline). Neither blocks Stage 5 entry.
metadata.worktree_state_devplatform: Working tree NOT clean: (a) `?? docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md` — dashboard maintenance artifact stale post-Phase-A (the 5-disabled-tasks include Dashboard Maintainer that updates this file; orphaned post-disabling). (b) `?? tools/vikunja/config.yml.pre_stage4_bak` — Stage 4.4 backup of vikunja config preserved for rollback; recommend Stage 5/6 hardening cleanup decision. (c) `?? tools/vikunja_mcp/bridge/inbox.json` — Vikunja bridge daemon scratch file for sandbox-agent mutations; normal runtime artifact, regenerated as needed. None block Stage 5 entry.
metadata.pytest_baseline: BlarAI side (`pytest shared/ services/ launcher/`): ~981 passed, 22 skipped per CLAUDE.md §Active State (post-Sprint-8 EA-5 baseline). UNCHANGED through Stage 4 (Stage 4 was BlarAI-side STATUS-only mutation + post-Phase-C state.json mutations not committed). devplatform side (`pytest --collect-only tools/`): 351 tests, 0 errors (unchanged from Stage 3 close 95884a0). Stage 4 added no new tests on devplatform side; cutover work was config repointing + script edits only.
metadata.stash_zero:
**CORRECTED 2026-04-29 post-emission per LA review.** Current stash list (verified 2026-04-29 post-handoff-emission via `git stash list`): - stash@{0}: On chore/platform-extraction: wake_launcher auto-stash co_lead_architect 20260429_004502 - stash@{1}: On chore/platform-extraction: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260429_004501 Both stashes are from 2026-04-29 (post-Phase-C wake_launcher firings; the 00:45 timestamp = midnight-local 2026-04-29). The "legendary" sdo 20260424_013002 stash that prior handoffs (G6→G7→G8) claimed was preserved through 13+-18+ cycles is **NO LONGER IN THE STASH LIST**. Reflog reveals a likely cause: commit `7ec424f fix(hygiene): Pattern D — auto-stash retention sweep (24h cap)` — auto-stash retention sweep appears to drop stashes older than 24h, which would have invalidated the multi-day preservation narrative post-Stage-0/1. Exact disappearance disposition is UNCERTAIN; bind as Anomaly A20 (forensic investigation deferred to Stage 6.7.5 Ticket forensic-A20).
Discipline going forward: **don't pop or apply ANY wake_launcher auto-stash** (whatever stash entries exist at any given moment). The discipline is generic, not pinned to a specific timestamp. dn10 is corrected accordingly below.
metadata.trigger_reason: la_request "Would now be a good time to launch the next Guide, Instance #9? Would it be best to create the new guide and have it create the plan for stage 5 and EA init prompts?" + LA's correction adding calibration-review responsibility ("After I spawn the new Guide instance, I will have you do an independent analysis of its initial response(s) to ensure calibration and understanding for the new Guide. Once I am sure it is ready to successfully complete stage 5, then you can retire."). Context: Stage 4 was HIGH-risk and consumed significant Guide-#8 context with 5-bug Phase C remediation + multiple subagent rounds + 116-atom ack chain bookkeeping; "successor benefits from a clean slate at high-risk boundaries." Stage 4 close also marked Stage 5's MEDIUM-risk entry — 4 destructive delete clusters across BlarAI/tools/ + BlarAI/docs/ require pre-flight scope alignment.

guide_handoff_brief.active_conversational_topic:
Stage 4 has just OPERATIONALLY CLOSED post-Phase-C remediation per the Guide-#8 closure record (commit ecc8e7c) supported by 1h45m soak verification (9:15→11:00 EDT, 32/32 wake-* WORK-GATE skip + 24/24 cadence_monitor silent + 0 fail-open claude invocations + LA-side runtime/platform isolation test). EA-8 has retired correctly. Stage 5 (Cleanup per docs/platform_separation/06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml — MEDIUM risk, single_session) NOT YET SPAWNED.
The LA's IMMEDIATE next steps per Guide-#9 spawn pattern: (1) LA spawns fresh Guide-#9 chat with: GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + STATUS.md + GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml + Variant B chat-init block + Stage 5 dispatch (06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml). (2) Guide-#9 runs Variant B comprehension gate (re-reads source-doc set per GUIDE_PROMPT.xml + this handoff brief + Guide-#7 verdict files at temp_for_responses/3.X + Guide-#8 dispatch artifacts at temp_for_responses/ — many for Stage 4: 4.0.v2 audit + n1/n2/n3 dispositions + Item-by-item disposition files + 4.13-CLOSURE + Phase B/C summaries). (3) Guide-#8 (me) reviews Guide-#9's initial response for calibration per LA's correction. Guide-#8 retires only after LA confirms Guide-#9 is ready to successfully complete Stage 5. (4) Guide-#9 considers Stage 5.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint (per established Guide-#7/#8 procedural inheritance). RECOMMENDED but NOT mandatory. (5) Guide-#9 drafts the Stage 5 EA-9 initialization pointer per the established pattern, AFTER LA resolves 3 open scope decisions (see d4 below). (6) LA spawns Stage 5 EA-9 with the init pointer + 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml + standard attachment set.
Guide-#9's IMMEDIATE substantive tasks (in order, after Variant B gate): (a) Acknowledge the Stage 4 close + flag any drift since 2026-04-29T15:30:00Z (compare branch_at_handoff_blarai ecc8e7c+1, branch_at_handoff_devplatform 751c3df, status_md_last_commit ecc8e7c, worktree state per metadata against actual repo state). (b) Read the Stage 5 dispatch (06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml) AND the 3 Stage 5 scope decisions identified by Guide-#8 (see d4). (c) Surface the 3 scope decisions to LA for resolution. Guide-#9 may propose option matrix; LA decides. (d) Decide on Stage 5.0.v2 audit checkpoint (recommended; lower stakes than Stage 4.0.v2 since Stage 5 is MEDIUM risk, but several scope decisions remain open). (e) If audit checkpoint chosen: author the Stage 5.0.v2 audit subagent prompt (model on Guide-#7's 3.0.v2 + Guide-#8's 4.0.v2 prompt structure); spawn it; synthesize findings into a Stage 5.0.v2 verdict (binding any spec amendments via cumulative-ack inheritance under new ack g9-ea9_n1). (f) Draft the Stage 5 EA-9 initialization pointer. (g) Provide LA with the file path of the init pointer + a short chat-paste message for the new Stage 5 EA-9 chat + the attachment list + the LA pre-prep checklist for Stage 5 spawn.

decision.title: Stage 4 closure structure: cutover commits + post-close Phase C remediation cycle
decision.what: Stage 4 closed via a multi-phase trajectory not seen in prior stages: - Initial cutover commits 2026-04-28: devplatform `df3d940` (Stage 4 cutover; Vikunja DB migrated; MCP repointed; 13/13 tasks re-registered) + `48df457` (runbook doc-drift sweep 4.12.A + 4.12.B) + `b9b213d` (post-stage unpause). BlarAI commit `5965f6b` (Stage 4 doc-drift sweep + STATUS Stage 4 COMPLETE marker per Items 4.12.A/B/C + 4.13). - Phase A 2026-04-29: 5 scheduled tasks DISABLED per LA directive (Welcome Back Poll, Daily Digest, Weekly Summary, Dashboard Maintainer, Toast Watchdog) — LA non-recognition of these tasks indicated they were never being used and posed cost-bleed/staleness risk. LA: "I don't even know what Welcome Back Poll, Daily Digest are. I have never seen one of those. If they are waking agents and costing money then we need to turn them off since they are not in use." - Phase B 2026-04-29: BUG 5 root-cause investigation (F-1 template-vs-literal mismatch in 5 PowerShell scripts that read .mcp.json directly via ConvertFrom-Json — VS Code MCP loader expands templates; ConvertFrom-Json does not). Risk-cost projection: ~$58 overnight cost bleed if not caught (wake_launcher fail-OPEN to claude on every wake-* firing). - Phase C 2026-04-29: Remediation execution — C1 .mcp.json template→literal revert (uncommitted, gitignored per Ticket I); C2 SKIPPED (BUG 3 reclassified N/A given C3 architecture); C3+C4 wake_launcher.ps1 path resolution fix at devplatform `be22a15` (lines 69 + 809 → $PSScriptRoot derivation, decoupled from -BlarAIRoot arg). 820 BlarAI logs migrated to devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/ for historical preservation pre-Stage-5. - Phase D 2026-04-29: Unpause + 1h45m soak verification 9:15→11:00 EDT. 32/32 wake-* firings clean (4 roles × 8 cycles). 24/24 cadence_monitor firings silent. 0 fail-open claude invocations. State markers stable. Runtime/platform isolation confirmed mid-soak by LA's BlarAI runtime launch + prompt response test. - Stage 4 closure record commit 2026-04-29: BlarAI `ecc8e7c` (this commit). STATUS.md updated per Stage 3 closure pattern. Cumulative-ack: 116 atoms (n3(l) added for Item 4.13 STATUS.md surgical truncate).
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9 inherits this multi-phase Stage 4 trajectory. Key absorption points: - The Phase C bug discovery pattern is a procedural inheritance — LA risk-check questions can prevent ~$58/day cost bleeds. Guide-#9 should similarly invite LA risk-checks at high-stakes Stage 5 decision points. - Phase D soak methodology (1h45m, 5/15-min cadence verification) is procedural inheritance for Stage 5 post-cleanup soak. Stage 5 dispatch's preconditions require "Fleet has executed scheduled tasks from devplatform successfully at least twice" — soak methodology satisfies this. - 5 disabled scheduled tasks affects Stage 5.9's verification command (which references `BlarAI_daily_digest` — DISABLED) — substitute decision needed (one of d4 scope decisions). - Cumulative-ack chain at Stage 4 close: 116 binding atoms. Guide-#9 emits new acks under `g9-ea9_n*` per global EA-numbering convention (per d2 + Anomaly A12 + dn30 inheritance).

decision.title: EA-numbering convention preservation: global, not stage-scoped
decision.what:
Per d2 of predecessor handoff: global EA-numbering convention continues across stages: Stage 1 = EA-1; Stage 2 = EA-2..EA-6; Stage 3 = EA-7; Stage 4 = EA-8; Stage 5 = EA-9. Each new EA continues the count regardless of stage boundary. Ack-naming convention follows: `g{guide_instance}-ea{ea_global_num}_n{seq}`.
DEFECT inheritance: Guide-#7 verdict files at temp_for_responses/3.X use stage-scoped `g7-ea1_n*` naming for the Stage 3 EA who is globally EA-7. Per no-amend doctrine on committed audit artifacts, those labels are NOT retroactively rewritten. Stage 4 verdicts (Guide-#8 era) used `g8-ea8_n*` correctly. Correction folds into Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A scope.
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#9 SHALL emit all new acks under `g9-ea9_n*` per global convention. Cumulative-ack chain at Stage 4 close (verbatim restatement required at Stage 5 EA-9 iter-1 gate): - g7-ea1_n10 (Iter 1 verdict; verbatim per no-amend, documented as A12) - g7-ea1_n11 (3.1.v2 escalation_watchdog filename; verbatim, A12) - g7-ea1_n12 (3.2.v2 selective governance copy + 4 hardening tickets; verbatim, A12) - g7-ea1_n13 (3.0.v2 audit checkpoint, 8 sub-clauses a-h; verbatim, A12) - g7-ea7_n14 (3.10 closure VERIFIED, 4 sub-clauses a-d; corrected naming) - g8-ea8_n1 (a..p) — Stage 4 preflight audit + LA decisions - g8-ea8_n2 (a..f) — workflow simplification (n2(c) S4U paste-relay carve-out) - g8-ea8_n3 (a..l) — execution-time spec refinements + n3(l) STATUS.md surgical truncate
EA-9 must restate the entire chain (now 116 binding atoms) verbatim at iter-1 comprehension gate. Anomaly A12 records the no-amend disposition; future stages CONTINUE to restate verbatim.

decision.title: Phase C remediation devplatform-only commit discipline
decision.what: Phase C remediation produced 4 devplatform commits (b9b213d unpause, be22a15 wake_launcher fix, plus pre-existing df3d940/48df457 cutover) but ZERO BlarAI commits. Reason: Phase C work was entirely devplatform-side; the C1 .mcp.json revert was NOT committed because .mcp.json is gitignored per Ticket I; state.json mutations were runtime data not committed. BlarAI HEAD remained at 5965f6b through Phase C remediation. BlarAI HEAD advances at Stage 4 closure record commit (ecc8e7c) and the subsequent Guide-#8 → Guide-#9 handoff XML commit (this).
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9 should expect Stage 5 work to advance BOTH BlarAI HEAD (multiple commits — file moves to .platform/, deletions, STATUS append, merge to main) AND devplatform HEAD (path-reference updates per Item 5.4 audit). Stage 5 is more BlarAI-side than Stage 4 was. The "BlarAI-side read-only invariant" that held through Stages 0-3 is RELAXED at Stage 5 by design — BlarAI/tools/ deletions + BlarAI/docs/ deletions + BlarAI/.platform/ creation + merge to main are all BlarAI-side.

decision.title: 3 OPEN Stage 5 SCOPE DECISIONS — LA must resolve BEFORE EA-9 init pointer drafting
decision.what:
The Stage 5 dispatch (06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml) was authored before post-Stage-4 state was known. Three scope decisions need LA resolution before Guide-#9 can author the EA-9 init pointer:
**Decision 1: Item 5.0.5 audit archive scope** — The dispatch archives logs from `BlarAI/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/` and other dirs slated for Stage 5.1 deletion. BUG 4 fix (Phase C3) already migrated 820 BlarAI logs to `devplatform/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/`. BlarAI side may now be empty or have only old pre-fix logs. Options: - Run archive anyway (no-op cost; preserves any straggler files) - Audit BlarAI/tools/scheduled-tasks/logs/ first; skip Item 5.0.5 if empty Recommendation: run Item 5.0.5 anyway; cost is minimal; preserves any non-log files that might be in the archived dirs (e.g., state.json siblings).
**Decision 2: Item 5.9 fleet end-to-end verification substitute** — The dispatch uses `Start-ScheduledTask -TaskName "BlarAI_daily_digest"` to verify fleet still operates from devplatform post-cleanup. But Daily Digest was DISABLED in Phase A 2026-04-29 per LA directive. Options: - Substitute with an active wake-* task (e.g., wait for `BlarAI_wake_sdo` natural firing every 15 min and verify LastTaskResult=0) - Drop Item 5.9 entirely (the 8 wake-* cycles in Phase D soak already proved end-to-end; Stage 5's deletions don't affect runtime path) - Re-enable Daily Digest temporarily for the verification, then re-Disable Recommendation: substitute with active wake-* (one cycle wait, 15 min max).
**Decision 3: Tickets II/JJ confirmation as Stage 6.7.5 deferred** — Tickets II (PS1 env-var refactor) and JJ (devplatform/.mcp.json template consistency) are bound to Stage 6.7.5. Stage 5 dispatch does not address them. Confirm: leave for 6.7.5? Or fold into Stage 5 scope as "while we're cleaning up"? Options: - DEFER (recommended) — Stage 5 scope is BlarAI-side cleanup; PS1 refactor + devplatform .mcp.json consistency are devplatform-side cleanup at 6.7.5 - FOLD INTO STAGE 5 — adds 30-45 min effort + small cross-side scope; not architecturally pure Recommendation: DEFER. Stage 5 has enough scope already (12+4 v2 work items).
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9 surfaces these 3 decisions to LA at session start (after Variant B comprehension gate). LA resolves; Guide-#9 records decisions in a Stage 5.0.v2 audit checkpoint verdict (or inline in the EA-9 init pointer if no audit checkpoint). EA-9 init pointer scope is contingent on these resolutions.

decision.title: Phase C remediation methodology as procedural inheritance for Stage 5 post-cleanup verification
decision.what:
Phase C surfaced 5 bugs post-cutover (BUGs 1/2/4/5 fixed; BUG 3 N/A given C3 architecture). The methodology for surfacing them was: (a) LA pointed risk-check question prevented overnight cost bleed; (b) Guide-#8 spawned subagents to investigate (Phase B); (c) remediation executed (Phase C); (d) soak verification (Phase D). Total bug-discovery-to-soak-clean: ~6 hours real time.
Stage 5 has analogous risk: 4 destructive delete clusters could leave references-to-deleted-paths in devplatform fleet tools that don't manifest until next firing. Item 5.4 dispatch already audits devplatform tools for path references but is verification-light.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9 should pre-plan for a post-Stage-5-deletion soak similar to Phase D. Recommend including in the EA-9 init pointer: (a) Post-Stage 5.1 + 5.5 deletion: brief verification (1-2 wake-* cycles) that fleet tools still execute without errors (b) Post-Stage 5.6 + 5.7 (pyproject + .gitignore updates): re-run pytest baseline check (Item 5.8) to catch any regression (c) Post-Item 5.10 commit (pre-merge HALT gate): MANDATORY verification that no devplatform fleet tool has stale BlarAI/tools/ path references The Phase D soak methodology (1h45m, observe 5/15-min cadence) is procedural inheritance; Guide-#9 may shorten or extend per LA judgment.

decision.title: Triple-subagent verification methodology continues for Stage 5 close (procedural inheritance)
decision.what:
Guide-#7 introduced triple-subagent verification at Stage 3 close (d6 of predecessor handoff). Guide-#8 SHOULD have applied it at Stage 4 close per dn28 inheritance, but DID NOT — Stage 4 close was supplanted by Phase C remediation discovery before triple-subagent verification could run. Phase D soak verification (1h45m, 32 wake-* + 24 cadence_monitor + 0 fail-open) substituted as the closure proof.
Stage 5 close warrants the original triple-subagent methodology. Stage 5 has multiple irreversible deletions; single-Guide review insufficient.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9 SHALL spawn three parallel verification subagents at Stage 5 close (when EA-9 reports completion at Item 5.12). Three orthogonal slices: - S1 (BlarAI-side technical correctness): all 7 platform tool dirs deleted, BlarAI/.platform/ populated, BlarAI/docs/ platform-doctrine docs deleted, .gitignore updated, pyproject.toml updated, BlarAI test baseline preserved (~981/22) - S2 (devplatform-side path-reference correctness): all devplatform fleet tools updated to look in BlarAI/.platform/ for coordination files (Item 5.4 audit complete), no stale BlarAI/tools/ references in any devplatform script, fleet still executes - S3 (procedural completeness): all 12+4 v2 sub-items executed, 8 exit_criteria met, audit archive (.platform/audit_archive/) intact, post-extract tag created, STATUS.md updated, merge to main HALT gate respected Convergence required across all three for VERIFIED. Synthesize into Stage 5 closure verdict (file: `temp_for_responses/5.12-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_9_STAGE5_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml`).

decision.title: Hardening followups remain NON-OPTIONAL (LA directive 2026-04-27, re-confirmed during Phase C)
decision.what: LA directive: hardening followups are NON-OPTIONAL for both documentation AND architectural cleanup (per dn26 inheritance). Phase C re-confirmed this — Tickets II + JJ + KK were bound as REQUIRED, not "optional" or "suggested". Stage 6.7.5 backlog is 20 tickets at this corrections commit (LL added post-handoff-emission per A20 — wake_launcher stash narrative correction). LA may triage priority at Stage 6 execution; existence is non-optional.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9 SHALL frame all Stage 5-discovered hardening items as REQUIRED Stage 6.7.5 tickets, NOT "optional" or "suggested". Add to backlog with clear acceptance criteria. Auto-memory feedback `feedback_doc_cleanup_non_optional.md` codifies this rule across all future sessions.

decision.title: Subagent verification discipline (A18 + Ticket X)
decision.what: During Stage 4 dispatch authoring, 3 separate subagents reported "source XMLs have BlarAI paths" (suggesting cutover incomplete or mis-authored) when actual `git diff` showed source XMLs were already migrated. Guide-#8 caught each false-negative by cross-checking subagent claims against actual `git diff` before accepting. No false-negative propagated into a committed dispatch.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9 SHALL adopt subagent verification discipline as procedural standard (per dn14/dn20/dn24 inheritance, now strengthened by A18). Never accept subagent claim about file state without `git diff` cross-check. Especially relevant for Stage 5 audit subagent (if spawned at Stage 5.0.v2): verify path-reference audit findings against actual file content.

decision.title: STATUS.md duplication root cause AMBIGUOUS — append discipline reminder
decision.what: Item 4.13 STATUS append produced duplicated "Stage 4 COMPLETE — 2026-04-28" content (each subsection 2x). Forensic root cause AMBIGUOUS — helper file 3,480 bytes single-copy, `[System.IO.File]::AppendAllText` logged once, diff showed duplicate addition. n3(l) directed surgical truncate at second marker.
decision.successor_instruction: Guide-#9: when EA-9 emits Item 5.12 STATUS append, spot-check the post-append STATUS.md for duplication BEFORE committing. The Stage 6.7.5 ticket on AppendAllText behavior + UTF-8 BOM intersection is REQUIRED but not yet investigated; Stage 5 should not be the first investigation. Use `Add-Content -NoNewline` or hash-verified append helper if possible.

decision.title: Stage 5.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint — RECOMMENDED but lower stakes than Stage 4.0.v2
decision.what:
Procedural inheritance from Guide-#7 (3.0.v2) and Guide-#8 (4.0.v2). Pre-flight audit converts in-stage compound drifts into pre-stage amendment cycles. Stage 5 is MEDIUM risk (single_session, 12+4 work items, multiple deletions); compound drifts less catastrophic than Stage 4's HIGH risk but still non-trivial.
Stage 5 has 3 KNOWN open scope decisions (d4) and 2-3 likely-drift areas: - Item 5.0.5 archive sourceDirs vs current BlarAI tools/ state (post-Phase-C log migration) - Item 5.9 verification task substitute (BlarAI_daily_digest disabled) - Item 5.4 audit findings (devplatform fleet tools paths to update — currently 3 file globs; may have grown post-Phase-C) - .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock gitignore handling (Stage 5.7 inline) - Stage 5.5 delete patterns may need review for any new platform-doctrine docs added during Stage 4 work
decision.successor_instruction:
Guide-#9: RECOMMENDATION run Stage 5.0.v2 audit checkpoint. Estimated runtime: 30-45 min (less than Stage 4.0.v2's 60-90 min because fewer sub-items). Bind amendments under new ack `g9-ea9_n1`. If LA declines (Option A — proceed without audit), document the decline.
AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO FULL AUDIT: Guide-#9 may inline-resolve the 3 known scope decisions (d4) + 2-3 likely-drift areas via single-pass Read of relevant files; this is faster (~15 min) but less rigorous. LA preference governs.

open_watchpoints.intro: All watchpoints inherited from Guide-#7 instance (17 watchpoints w1-w17). Status updates per Guide-#8 instance + new watchpoints from Stage 4 + Phase C work.
open_watchpoints.watchpoint: Agents Cadence Monitor LogonType=S4U; Stage 4.7 Unregister + Register. RESOLVED at Stage 4 Item 4.8 via n3(k) elevation paste-relay carve-out. State=Ready post-Stage-4. Procedure successful. A3 / rr5
G-2 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory rewrite at Stage 4.7. Stage 4 XML had the regex fix; verified at Stage 4 close. G-2

Vikunja stop precondition at Stage 4.1. Stopped successfully; cutover proceeded. V4=0 invariant restored ahead of cutover; restored to V4=1 post-cutover (Vikunja running for normal operations). g6-ea6_n8 / V4-closure-drift / rr6

F-D1-1 MCP-bus class-method routing migration: when VikunjaClient HTTP delegation lands at Stage 5+ cleanup. Stage 6.7.5-pattern DevPlatform-Meta hardening ticket already in scope. NOT a Stage 5 concern unless Item 5.4 audit surfaces specific path references. g6-ea6_n7 / F-D1-1 / D4-DIVERGE-forward
blarai_auxiliary_projects 4 / 7 / 9 investigation at Stage 6 (per Anomaly A10 + Stage 6 hardening directive).
A11 alias-vs-rename cleanup (rename --blarai-root → --project-root with deprecation warning). Per dn26: REQUIRED, not optional.

VIKUNJA_PASS literal rotation (F-1). Phase C reverted BlarAI/.mcp.json to literal (uncommitted, gitignored). Tickets II + JJ refactor 5 PS1 scripts to direct env-var pattern (Python parity), then template can return.

watchpoint.recheck_at: before any git stash pop / forever

open_watchpoints.watchpoint: **Wake_launcher auto-stash discipline (corrected from prior multi-cycle preservation narrative).** Current stash list (verified 2026-04-29 post-handoff-emission): - stash@{0}: On chore/platform-extraction: wake_launcher auto-stash co_lead_architect 20260429_004502 - stash@{1}: On chore/platform-extraction: wake_launcher auto-stash sdo 20260429_004501 The original sdo 20260424_013002 stash that prior handoffs (G6→G7→G8) claimed was preserved through 13+/18+ cycles is **NO LONGER IN THE STASH LIST**. Reflog suggests the Pattern D auto-stash retention sweep (commit 7ec424f, 24h cap) auto-dropped it. Discipline going forward: **don't pop or apply ANY wake_launcher auto-stash** at any point — this is generic, not pinned to a specific timestamp. ANOMALY A20 captures the disappearance + corrects the prior preservation narrative.
Worktree precondition (`git worktree list` empty) re-verify at Stage 4.0.5. Verified clean at Stage 4 entry. rr7

No merge to chore/platform-extraction → main until LA explicitly approves per R10. Merge gate at Stage 5.10 with explicit Lead Architect approval. Stage 5 dispatch Item 5.10 has explicit HALT for this.

watchpoint.recheck_at: every EA report at every cycle

open_watchpoints.watchpoint: EA-paraphrase-introduced-error pattern. Re-confirmed in EA-8 across multiple Phase C reports — close-report under-enumeration of anomalies, narrative paraphrase drift from disposition files. Mature response: independent disk verification on every EA report; never trust paraphrased narratives without checking disposition files OR comparing close-report claims to actual git diff stats.

Single-commit discipline. Stage 5 has multiple commits by design (Item 5.10 commit + Item 5.11 tags + Item 5.12 STATUS append; possibly intermediate commits per the dispatch). Mid-stage interim commits OUTSIDE the dispatch's prescribed commit blocks are NOT allowed.

Pre-flight Stage 5.0.v2 audit checkpoint. Stage 5 is MEDIUM risk (vs Stage 4 HIGH); audit recommended but not strictly required. d10 elaborates trade-offs (full audit vs inline-resolve). LA preference governs.

Triple-subagent verification at Stage 5 close (d6). Stage 5 has multiple irreversible deletions; single-Guide review insufficient. Three orthogonal subagents (S1 BlarAI-side technical, S2 devplatform-side path-reference, S3 procedural) at Stage 5.12 close.
devplatform working-tree drift (`M .gitignore` 7-line tail duplication) at Stage 3 close. Resolved at Stage 4.0 preflight (Item 4.0). rr8 / S1 finding A2

devplatform `tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` BlarAI runtime pause-state snapshot. Resolved at Stage 4 Item 4.13.5 (state.resume_fleet) + Phase C4 (wake_launcher reads devplatform state.json authoritatively). rr3 / S1 finding A1
EA-numbering convention defect in committed Guide-#7 verdict files (g7-ea1_n10..n13). Per no-amend doctrine; A12 records; Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A absorbs correction. A12 / d2 / Ticket A

Three open Stage 5 scope decisions (d4) MUST be resolved BEFORE Guide-#9 drafts the EA-9 init pointer. (1) Item 5.0.5 archive scope vs Phase-C-migrated logs; (2) Item 5.9 verification substitute given Daily Digest disabled; (3) Tickets II/JJ confirmation as 6.7.5 deferred. d4 / Stage 5 dispatch staleness

Phase D soak methodology procedural inheritance. Recommend post-Stage-5-deletion soak (1-2 wake-* cycles minimum) to verify devplatform fleet tools still execute without errors after deletions. d5 elaborates.
`.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` mutex file appears in BlarAI worktree per wake_launcher. Stage 5.7 should add it to .gitignore. Currently untracked but harmless. w20 / Stage 5.7 gitignore augment

`BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` is a DEAD ARTIFACT post-Phase-C4 (paused; not read by wake_launcher). Auto-resolved at Stage 5.1 directory deletion per Ticket KK. EA-9 should NOT attempt to "clean up" this state.json before Stage 5.1 — that would be redundant and could trigger fleet-pause SOP confusion. Just delete the parent dir per dispatch. Ticket KK / w21

F-1 fix (`.mcp.json` template) created cross-cutting bug for 5 PS1 scripts that read .mcp.json directly via ConvertFrom-Json. Phase C reverted to literal as fastest fix; architectural cleanup deferred to II/JJ (Stage 6.7.5). KEY LESSON: `.mcp.json` is read by THREE different consumers (VS Code MCP loader, PowerShell ConvertFrom-Json, Python config-loader) with different template-expansion behavior. Future config-format changes must verify across all three. BUG 5 / II + JJ / A16

anomalies_not_yet_in_status.intro: STATUS.md is fully updated through ecc8e7c (Stage 4 close). A15-A19 are recorded; the 8-item Stage 4 Closure Footnotes section captures all dispositions including Phase C remediation. Guide-#9 inherits a clean STATUS state, with one new anomaly (A20) bound below post-handoff-emission for STATUS.md addition at Stage 5 close.
anomalies_not_yet_in_status.anomaly:
**wake_launcher auto-stash narrative correction.** LA review of handoff XML (8468f8f) flagged that the metadata `` block + watchpoint w8 + prohibition dn10 were tracking a non-existent artifact. Verification on disk (2026-04-29): - `git stash list` returns 2 entries, both 2026-04-29 dated (post-Phase-C wake_launcher firings) - The "legendary" sdo 20260424_013002 stash that prior handoffs (G6→G7→G8) claimed was preserved through 13+/18+ cycles is **NOT** in the stash list - Reflog reveals commit `7ec424f fix(hygiene): Pattern D — auto-stash retention sweep (24h cap)` — auto-stash retention sweep that drops stashes older than 24h The most likely disposition: Pattern D auto-stash retention sweep auto-dropped the legendary stash at some unknown point post-Stage-0/1. The multi-cycle preservation narrative was structurally invalid past 24h. Carryforward correction: don't-pop discipline applies generically to whatever stash entries exist, not to a specific timestamp.
Forensic uncertainty: exact stash disappearance moment + reflog trace not investigated (would require traversing many reflog entries to identify when sdo 20260424_013002 stopped appearing). Stage 6.7.5 forensic-A20 ticket bound for the investigation. NOT blocking Stage 5; just a narrative correction.
Action for Guide-#9: include A20 entry in Stage 5 close STATUS append (Item 5.12 STATUS append). Until then, A20 lives only in this handoff XML.

la_profile.intro: All 21 traits from instance 7→8 brief carry forward. Re-confirmed during Guide-#8 work. Three new observations as p22 + p23 + p24.
la_profile.trait: Non-dev "vibe coder". Re-confirmed throughout Stage 4.
Prefers XML-formatted Guide-to-EA directives via temp_for_responses/.
**"Mature not minimal" motto.** Re-confirmed during Phase C investigation depth.
Tables faster than prose for status/comparison.
Forensic analysis discipline; appreciates independent disk verification on EA reports. Drove Phase C's BUG 1 root cause investigation via continued pointed questioning.
Paste-attachment hazards. Worked correctly for n3(k) S4U paste-relay carve-out at Item 4.8.

Decision-asks calibrated to scope/impact (not technical implementation). Held throughout Phase C — LA decided Phase A scope (5 tasks to disable) + Phase C Option A (literal revert), did NOT request technical implementation details.
Comprehension-gate discipline. Required at every fresh Guide/EA spawn.

Risk-check questioning. **CRITICAL — prevented ~$58 overnight cost bleed at Phase C BUG 5 discovery.** LA's question "We should only see the powershell and python scripts running but no claude instances, is that correct?" forced Phase B investigation that surfaced BUG 5.
Soak preference: shorter when system cadence is high. LA challenged 24h soak recommendation given 5/15-min fleet cadence; agreed 75-min soak captures equivalent stability.

Calibration-review request pattern. NEW from Guide-#7 era; reaffirmed at Guide-#8 → Guide-#9 transition. LA requires Guide-#8 (predecessor) to review Guide-#9 (successor)'s initial responses before predecessor retires.
Cross-session memory awareness. LA references prior session decisions; expects auto-memory to capture.
Acronym-spell-out preference (per memory feedback_spell_out_acronyms.md). First use of any acronym should be expanded.
Vikunja webUI for manual ticket work; MCP for automated.
Long XML/structured outputs to workspace file (per memory feedback_long_outputs_to_file.md). Don't dump >30 lines of XML to chat.
Multi-file entrypoint verification discipline. EA reports must be cross-checked against actual disk state.
Recursive verification via subagents at high-risk boundaries. "Mature not minimal" = subagent triple-check.
No commendations of agents (per memory feedback_no_commendations.md).
Pause fleet for substantive git work (per memory feedback_pause_fleet_for_git_work.md).
VIKUNJA_TOKEN obtained pre-Stage-4-spawn per Stage 4.3 dependency.
All AI dev surfaces closed pre-Stage-4-spawn (Claude Desktop, VS Code, Claude Code; predecessor Stage 4 EA-8 was VS Code Copilot only).

**Cost-mitigation pragmatism.** LA disabled 5 scheduled tasks per non-recognition signal at Phase A — preferred turning OFF unused features over investigating their value. Pattern: when LA doesn't recognize a feature, default to disabling rather than re-enabling-with-investigation.

**Soak-duration empirical reasoning.** LA challenged 24h soak recommendation by analyzing the system's actual cadence (5/15-min). Pattern: LA expects guides to justify recommendations against the actual operating profile, not generic spec language.

**Calibration-review-before-retirement explicit pattern.** Reaffirmed in Guide-#8 → Guide-#9 transition. Predecessor Guide must validate successor Guide's calibration on initial responses before retiring. Memory feedback to update.

active_risk_ids.intro: Risk IDs that proved active during Guide-#8 work. Carryforward + status updates + new from Phase C.
active_risk_ids.risk: Worktree contamination. Re-verified clean at every Stage 4 cycle.
S4U elevation; resolved via n3(k) paste-relay carve-out at Item 4.8.
resume_fleet vs unpause_fleet. Function-name discipline held; correct calls used throughout Stage 4.
Stage 4.7 false-green via bare WorkingDirectory. XML regex fix verified at Stage 4 close.
$BLARAI_PID resolution.
VIKUNJA_PASS literal — devplatform side env-var substituted per n13(c); BlarAI side reverted to literal in Phase C1 (uncommitted, gitignored). Stage 6.7.5 II + JJ refactor scope finalizes.
Vikunja label-ID drift.
Multi-BlarAI-projects discovery; Stage 6.7.5 finalization.
REST vs MCP for Vikunja access.
Per dn14 + p16. EA-8 also exhibited paraphrase-introduced-error in Phase C reports.
Multi-instance now (EA-7 + EA-8). See w11.
Stopped at Stage 4.1; restarted post-cutover for normal operations.
Stage 3 acks all SATISFIED. Cumulative-ack chain extended to 116 atoms at Stage 4 close.

Stage 4 had 0 in-stage compound drifts (audit-checkpoint methodology worked). HOWEVER, Phase C uncovered 5 post-cutover bugs that spec didn't anticipate. FORWARD: Stage 5 likely to exhibit similar pattern via Item 5.4 audit findings; pre-flight Stage 5.0.v2 audit recommended (d10 + w13).
Carry-forward from Stage 3. New g8-ea8_n* used correctly throughout Stage 4. Stage 6.7.5 TICKET A absorbs prior correction.
Pre-flight audit pattern. d10 elaborates Stage 5.0.v2 trade-offs.
Defense-in-depth audit at Stage 5 close per d6.

Post-cutover bug discovery from cross-cutting config changes (F-1 .mcp.json template). 5 PS1 scripts affected. Generalize: any config-format change needs verification across all consumer types (VS Code, PowerShell, Python).
1h45m soak verification with 5/15-min cadence observation. Procedural inheritance for Stage 5 post-cleanup soak (d5).
Item 4.13 STATUS append produced duplicate; surgical truncate (n3(l)) resolved. Root cause not identified. See d9 + A17.
3 subagents reported false negatives during Stage 4 dispatch authoring. See d8 + A18 + Ticket X.
LA's pointed risk-check question prevented ~$58/day cost bleed at Phase C. See p9 + A16.

source_doc_revisions.intro: Source-doc revisions during Guide-#8 lifetime. STATUS.md updated; all 7 stage XMLs + MASTER_PLAN + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW + RECOVERY + INFRA_DELTA_v2 + ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE UNCHANGED. ~30+ Guide-#8 dispatch artifacts added to docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/.

revision.commits: ecc8e7c (Stage 4 close STATUS append: STATUS.md alone +121/-8; commit aggregate +409/-8 across STATUS.md + 2 new XMLs at temp_for_responses/; Stage 4 Closure Footnotes 8 items; A15-A19 anomalies; Stage Ledger Stage 4 row PENDING → COMPLETE; Current State table updated; Phase C addendum to Stage 4 COMPLETE marker; 2 Execution Log entries)
revision.note: STATUS.md is now 483 lines (was 370 pre-Stage-4-close). All Stage 4 + Phase C dispositions documented in the 8-item Closure Footnotes set. Append-only discipline preserved; A1-A14 untouched; Recovery Anchors / Vikunja Project IDs / How to Resume sections untouched.

revision.commits: 76+ commits across Stage 4 EA-8 init pointer + multiple Item-by-item dispositions + Phase B/C dispatch artifacts
revision.files_added_this_instance_partial_list: Key files for Guide-#9 to read: 1. 2c6be78 + 134e17a + 9d5fcd8 + 13231f0 + 75a73ab — Stage 4.0.v2 audit checkpoint + EA-8 init pointer evolution + supplements 2. PHASE_B_FINDINGS_PLUS_PHASE_C_OPTIONS.xml (committed at ecc8e7c) — Phase B BUG 5 root-cause investigation + 3 Phase C options 3. PHASE_C_CLOSURE_SUMMARY.xml (committed at ecc8e7c) — Phase C remediation summary + soak verification setup 4. (multiple Item-by-item disposition files for Items 4.0 through 4.13 — see git log on chore/platform-extraction)
revision.note: Guide-#9 reads these as part of Variant B comprehension gate plus all instance-7 artifacts (4 Guide-#7 verdict files at 3.X) plus all instance-6 artifacts (16 Guide-#6 disposition files) plus 9 Guide-#5 disposition files plus 5 EA mid-stage handoffs at ea_outputs/. Significant audit-chain reading; plan ~60-90 minutes of disciplined reading.

revision.status: moderate — Stage 4 cutover commits + Phase C remediation
revision.commits: devplatform `df3d940` (Stage 4 cutover, parent 95884a0) + `48df457` (doc-drift sweep) + `b9b213d` (post-stage unpause) + `be22a15` (Phase C wake_launcher fix) + `751c3df` (handoff-prep pause). 5 commits since Stage 3 close.
revision.note: devplatform now authoritative for: Vikunja MCP server, scheduled-task scripts (with $PSScriptRoot derivation post-Phase-C4), autonomy_budget state, fleet_observability, fleet_ops, gate_stale_cleaner. BlarAI workspace + .platform/ + Vikunja DB (at LocalAppData) preserved per LA n1(c).

source_doc_revisions.revision_no_change:
No changes to 7 stage XMLs (01_STAGE0_PREFLIGHT through 07_STAGE6_HARDENING), 00_MASTER_PLAN.md, AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW.md, WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW.md, RECOVERY.md, VERIFICATION_COMMANDS.md, INFRA_DELTA_v2.md, ROLLBACK_NOVICE_GUIDE.md, GUIDE_PROMPT.xml, GUIDE_USAGE.md, CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, devplatform/projects/registry.yaml, BlarAI/.platform/vikunja_project_ids.yaml.
Stage 6.7.5 hardening backlog is now 20 tickets: I, K, L, M, V, W, X, Y, Z, AA, BB, CC, EE, FF, GG, HH, II, JJ, KK, LL (plus carryforward TICKET A from Stage 3 g7-ea1 → g7-ea7 naming defect). Ticket LL added post-handoff-emission per Anomaly A20 — wake_launcher auto-stash multi-cycle preservation narrative correction + forensic investigation of stash disappearance via Pattern D 24h retention sweep.

first_action_for_successor.step: After your Variant B comprehension gate is complete, briefly acknowledge the handoff and flag any STATUS drift or git advance since 2026-04-29T15:30:00Z. Compare branch_at_handoff_blarai (chore/platform-extraction @ ecc8e7c+1 — handoff XML commit), branch_at_handoff_devplatform (main @ 751c3df), status_md_last_commit (ecc8e7c), worktree_state_blarai (`M tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` + `?? .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock` per w20/w21), worktree_state_devplatform (`?? docs/FLEET_DASHBOARD.md` + `?? tools/vikunja/config.yml.pre_stage4_bak`) against actual repo state. Expected new commits since this handoff: only the LA's commit of GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml itself (this file).

**Read Guide-#8's audit-chain artifacts in addition to the Variant B mandatory reads:** all files at docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/ that begin with "4." (Stage 4 era, Guide-#8). Plus all instance-7 artifacts (4 Guide-#7 verdict files at 3.X + 2 audit-evidence MDs at 3.0.v2 + 3.10). Plus all instance-6 artifacts. Plus 9 Guide-#5 disposition files. Plus 5 EA mid-stage handoffs at ea_outputs/. Plus the 2 Phase B/C dispatch artifacts (PHASE_B_FINDINGS_PLUS_PHASE_C_OPTIONS.xml + PHASE_C_CLOSURE_SUMMARY.xml).
Total audit-chain reading: ~30 (Guide-#8 Stage 4) + 4 (Guide-#7) + 16 (Guide-#6) + 9 (Guide-#5) + 5 (EA mid-stage handoffs) + 2 (Phase B/C dispatches) = ~66 files. Significant reading overhead; plan ~60-90 minutes of disciplined reading.

**Surface the 3 Stage 5 scope decisions to LA** (per d4). LA resolves; Guide-#9 records decisions. These MUST be resolved BEFORE Guide-#9 drafts the EA-9 init pointer. Recommended order: surface as a single matrix; allow LA to give 3 yes/no decisions in one response.

**DECIDE on Stage 5.0.v2 pre-flight audit checkpoint** (per d10 + w13). Stage 5 is MEDIUM risk; full audit recommended but not strictly required. Alternative: inline-resolve the 3 d4 decisions + 2-3 likely-drift areas via single-pass Read of relevant files. LA preference governs. If full audit chosen: - Author the audit subagent prompt (model on `temp_for_responses/3.0.v2-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_7_STAGE3_COMPREHENSIVE_AUDIT_CHECKPOINT.xml` and Guide-#8's Stage 4.0.v2 prompt structure) - Spawn the subagent (general-purpose; thoroughness very thorough) - Subagent scope: all 12+4 v2 Stage 5 sub-items vs actual disk state; verify Item 5.4 path-reference audit comprehensive; check Stage 5.5 delete patterns vs current BlarAI/docs/ state - Output: drift inventory by item, classified by severity (Class A/B/C/D) - Synthesize into Stage 5.0.v2 verdict; bind amendments under new ack g9-ea9_n1 via cumulative-ack inheritance - Commit verdict + evidence MD If LA declines (Option A — proceed without audit), document the decline in your first_action_for_successor outcome and proceed to step 5 directly.

**Draft the Stage 5 EA-9 initialization pointer XML** following the same pattern as `temp_for_responses/4.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_8_STAGE4_CUTOVER.xml` (Guide-#8 era pattern). Adapt for: - EA instance N (EA-9 per global EA-numbering convention; see d2) - Stage 5 entry per docs/platform_separation/06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml + 3 d4 scope decisions resolved - Required reading order: 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml + STATUS.md (post-ecc8e7c) + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + Guide-#7 verdict files + Guide-#8 Stage 4 disposition files + Phase B/C dispatches + audit-evidence MDs + AUDIT_RISK_REVIEW + WORKFLOW_INTEGRITY_REVIEW + INFRA_DELTA_v2 + RECOVERY + 00_MASTER_PLAN + (if Stage 5.0.v2 audit done) Stage 5.0.v2 verdict + audit-evidence MD - Required first actions: (a) Verify HEAD ecc8e7c+1 + worktree state per w20/w21; (b) V0 pickup verification (HEAD blarai, HEAD devplatform = 751c3df, working tree state, pytest baselines, current stash list state per <stash_zero> block, state.json invariants, fleet paused, MCP configs all point to devplatform); (c) iteration-1 comprehension gate emission - Stage 5 work scope per 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml: Item 5.0.5 audit archive + 5.1 platform tool deletion + 5.2 .platform creation + 5.3 BlarAI coordination file moves + 5.4 devplatform path-reference update + 5.5+5.5.v2 platform-doctrine doc deletion + 5.6 pyproject update + 5.7 .gitignore update (incl .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock per w20) + 5.8 test baseline check + 5.9 fleet verification (substituted per d4 decision 2) + 5.10 commit + HALT for LA approval + 5.11 tags + 5.12 STATUS append - Carry-forward acks: 116 atoms all SATISFIED at appropriate Item closures; new acks g9-ea9_n* per global convention - Forward-active items: w4-w14 (relevant subset) + w18-w22 (Stage 5 specific) Naming convention: `docs/platform_separation/temp_for_responses/5.0-EA_INITIALIZATION_INSTANCE_9_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml`

Provide the LA with: (a) the file path of the init pointer; (b) a short chat-paste message for the new Stage 5 EA-9 chat ("Execute the attached..."); (c) the attachment list (analogous to the Stage 4 attachment list, plus Guide-#8 era artifacts + the new Stage 5 init pointer + 06_STAGE5_CLEANUP.xml + 4 Guide-#7 verdict files + audit-evidence MDs + (if produced) Stage 5.0.v2 verdict + evidence + 5 EA mid-stage handoffs + Guide-#6 + Guide-#5 dispositions + Phase B/C dispatches); (d) the LA pre-prep checklist for Stage 5 spawn (no special tokens needed; AI surfaces closed; no elevated PowerShell required (Stage 5 does NOT touch S4U tasks); ~2-4 hour real-time block expected).

Wait for the LA to spawn the Stage 5 EA-9 + paste back the Stage 5 EA-9 iteration-1 comprehension gate. Review per the GUIDE_PROMPT.xml ea_comprehension_gate_review procedure. APPROVED authorizes EA-9 to begin Item 5.0.5. Then ongoing review of Stage 5 work + STOP dispositions if compound drifts surface.
LA's calibration-review pattern: Guide-#8 (predecessor — me) reviews Guide-#9's initial response(s) for calibration before retiring. LA explicitly requested: "After I spawn the new Guide instance, I will have you do an independent analysis of its initial response(s) to ensure calibration and understanding for the new Guide. Once I am sure it is ready to successfully complete stage 5, then you can retire."
IMPORTANT: Guide-#9's initial response will be reviewed by Guide-#8 (predecessor). Guide-#9 should optimize for clear demonstration of Variant B comprehension gate completeness, calibrated reading of this handoff XML, and understanding of the 3 d4 scope decisions.

**At Stage 5 close** (when EA-9 reports completion at Item 5.12), spawn three parallel verification subagents per d6 + w14. Three orthogonal slices: - S1 (BlarAI-side technical correctness): all 7 platform tool dirs deleted, BlarAI/.platform/ populated correctly, BlarAI/docs/ platform-doctrine docs deleted per Stage 5.5+5.5.v2 patterns, .gitignore updated incl .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock, pyproject.toml updated, BlarAI test baseline preserved (~981/22 per CLAUDE.md §Active State) - S2 (devplatform-side path-reference correctness): Item 5.4 audit findings applied — all devplatform fleet tools updated to look in BlarAI/.platform/ for coordination files, no stale BlarAI/tools/ references in any devplatform script, fleet still executes successfully - S3 (procedural completeness): all 12+4 v2 sub-items executed, 8 exit_criteria met, audit archive (.platform/audit_archive/) intact for forensic recovery, post-extract tag created, STATUS.md updated, merge to main HALT gate respected Convergence required across all three for VERIFIED. Synthesize into Stage 5 closure verdict (file: `temp_for_responses/5.12-GUIDE_RESPONSE_INSTANCE_9_STAGE5_CLOSE_VERIFIED.xml`).

prime_stage_5_init_pointer_watchpoints.point: Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must restate ALL 116 binding acks VERBATIM with status flags. Do NOT summarize or paraphrase. EA-9 must restate them in iteration-1 gate.
Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must explicitly enumerate w18-w22 (Stage-5-specific watchpoints) VERBATIM. NOT just by reference to handoff file.
Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must include the 3 d4 scope decisions (resolved by LA) + any Stage 5.0.v2 audit findings as binding amendments under g9-ea9_n1.

Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must include EXPLICIT pre-prep checklist for LA: (a) close all AI dev surfaces (Claude Desktop, VS Code, Claude Code) before spawning EA-9; (b) prepare for ~2-4 hour real-time block; (c) NO special tokens needed (no VIKUNJA_TOKEN required for Stage 5); (d) NO elevated PowerShell required (Stage 5 does not touch S4U tasks).
Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must include the V0 pickup verification block (current state.json paused per `751c3df`; HEAD ecc8e7c+1; etc.). EA-9's iteration-1 gate must report V0 results.

Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must explicitly forward the EA-paraphrase-error pattern as a discipline reminder (per w11). EA-7 + EA-8 both exhibited under-enumeration of anomalies in close report. Mature response is independent disk verification + close-report cross-check against actual git diff stats.

Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer should reference the 8-item Stage 4 Closure Footnotes in STATUS.md as the canonical source for Stage 4 + Phase C disposition narratives (NOT EA-8's chat-narrative paraphrases per w11).

Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must include explicit STOP-and-report instructions for: (a) any deletion that would remove unexpected content (sanity-check Stage 5.1 sourceDirs match expected pre-delete inventory); (b) any pyproject.toml or .gitignore mod that would affect tests not in scope; (c) any path reference in devplatform tools that points to a now-deleted BlarAI/tools/ subdir (Item 5.4 audit completeness gate).

Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must include the audit-checkpoint methodology and triple-subagent verification methodology as procedural inheritance — EA-9 should expect Guide-#9 to spawn subagents at appropriate points; EA-9 cooperates without confusion.

Stage 5 EA-9 init pointer must include the Phase C BUG-discovery pattern as a watchpoint — post-deletion, brief verification soak (1-2 wake-* cycles) recommended before Item 5.10 commit. d5 elaborates.

do_not_list.intro: Carry forward all 30 prohibitions from instance 7→8 brief (dn1-dn30). Add 5 new (dn31-dn35) for instance 8 work products + Stage 5 specifics.
do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT re-chase STATUS.md line 11 v2-banner HEAD staleness. Known chicken-and-egg.
Pre-execution invariant past-tense issue resolved at Stage 1 EA close.
Do NOT edit, or allow any Stage 4-5 EA to edit, BlarAI's CLAUDE.md, .github/copilot-instructions.md, or AGENTS.md. V8 / 6.1.v2 deferred.
Do NOT confuse fleet pause with Vikunja server stop. Different concerns.
Do NOT relax acknowledgment-wait for Stage 5 EA-9. Strict verbatim enforcement of 116-ack chain.
Do NOT debug a "physform-extraction" typo.
Do NOT regenerate or revise Stage 0 / Stage 1 / Stage 2 / Stage 3 / Stage 4 baseline JSON / exit reports / mid-stage handoffs / closure verdicts. Frozen audit artifacts.
Do NOT emit a handoff after only a partial Stage UNLESS LA-requested OR per i1-4 trigger.
Do NOT modify this handoff brief or any predecessor handoff brief in git history. Permanent records.

Do NOT allow any Stage 5 EA to run `git stash pop` / `git stash apply` on **any wake_launcher auto-stash entry** (stash@{N} regardless of timestamp). Prior handoffs pinned this prohibition to the legendary `sdo 20260424_013002` stash; that stash is no longer in the list (per A20 correction). The discipline applies generically to all wake_launcher auto-stashes.
Do NOT delete, modify, or "improve" GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_DIRECTIVE.xml or GUIDE_POST_STAGE1_CLEANUP_BRIEF.md. Audit artifacts.
Do NOT assume LA's file-write permission carries forward across instances.
Do NOT delete or "clean up" the Guide disposition files in temp_for_responses/.
Do NOT trust the EA's "I did X" reports without disk-level verification. Both EA-7 and EA-8 exhibited under-enumeration. Future EA-9 likely similar.
Do NOT accept a partial-commit Stage-N mid-stage handoff. Mid-stage handoff XMLs are docs-only.
Do NOT silently approve scope expansion. Default disposition for any audit-surfaced PS1 is no-action with Appendix A.1 citation.
Do NOT shortcut analysis to save context window. "Mature not minimal." Depth wins over speed.
Do NOT confuse the legacy fleet_observability/_vikunja_client.py with the new chokepoint tools/_vikunja_client.py.
Do NOT defer in-scope defect closures to a different stage when the fix is trivial.
Do NOT skip the "mature not minimal" disk-verification step on EA reports that look clean.
Do NOT spawn Stage 5 EA-9 without first authoring the Stage 5 EA-9 initialization pointer.
Do NOT skip the Variant B comprehension gate for Guide-#9 (or any successor).
Do NOT modify the GUIDE_HANDOFF_LATEST.xml file once it's committed. Audit anchor.
Do NOT skip independent disk verification on Stage 5 EA-9 narrative summaries.
Do NOT silently correct the EA-numbering defect in committed Guide-#7 verdict files. No-amend doctrine.
Do NOT mark Stage 6.7.5 hardening tickets as "optional" or "suggested". REQUIRED per LA directive.

prohibition.status_change: downgraded_to_MED — Stage 5 risk lower than Stage 4

do_not_list.prohibition: Do NOT skip the pre-flight Stage 5.0.v2 audit checkpoint without explicit LA decline. Recommended; LA may decline given Stage 5 MEDIUM (vs Stage 4 HIGH) risk.
Do NOT skip triple-subagent verification at Stage 5 close. Multiple irreversible deletions; convergence required.
Do NOT spawn Stage 5 EA-9 without LA pre-prep verification (close AI surfaces; ~2-4h block; no special tokens).
Do NOT propagate the EA-1 vs EA-7 naming defect into NEW Guide-#9 verdicts. Use g9-ea9_n* for new acks; verbatim restate prior chain.

**Do NOT delete `BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json` directly.** Per w21 + Ticket KK: this is a dead artifact post-Phase-C4. Stage 5.1 deletes the parent directory which auto-resolves it. Direct deletion before Stage 5.1 risks fleet-pause SOP confusion. Just let Stage 5.1 run.

**Do NOT execute Stage 5.1 deletions before Item 5.0.5 audit archive completes.** S-2 fix in dispatch is mandatory — archive provides forensic recovery if anything goes wrong. Item ordering is 5.0.5 → 5.1 (NOT 5.1 → 5.0.5).

**Do NOT skip the post-Phase-C state.json verification step at Stage 5 entry.** Verify devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json shows fleet_paused=true (handoff prep pause `751c3df`) AND BlarAI/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json shows fleet_paused=true (dead artifact, last_updated_by from BUG 5 emergency re-pause). Both should be paused at Stage 5 entry; EA-9 unpause at Stage 5 close per dispatch.

**Do NOT defer .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock to a "later" gitignore add.** Per w20: it's untracked now and showing up in every Stage 4 EA-8 report's PREFLIGHT git_status output. Stage 5.7 .gitignore augment MUST include this. Eliminates noise from future PREFLIGHT diffs.

**Do NOT propagate Phase C remediation lessons into Stage 5 dispatch silently.** If Item 5.4 audit surfaces path-reference issues that mirror BUG 1's $RepoRoot vs $PSScriptRoot pattern (e.g., other PS1 scripts using $RepoRoot for state/logs), document as new finding under g9-ea9_n* AND consider whether they need to be fixed as part of Stage 5 or deferred to 6.7.5. Do NOT silently fix in-place.
